title: How to count cells greater than a value
task: Count how many cells pass a numeric threshold, like scores over 80 or orders over $100.

excel.note: The comparison goes in quotes. Use ">=" for at-least, "<" for below.

explanation: COUNTIF's criterion is a text string, so the comparison operator and number go together in quotes: ">100". In the sample, only 150 and 200 exceed 100, so the count is 2 — note 100 itself is excluded; use ">=100" to include it. To reference a cell as the threshold, concatenate: =COUNTIF(A2:A6,">"&C1).
